title: centaurea gallica var. micrantha
intro: Centaurea gallica var. micrantha, also known as the small-flowered cornflower, is a subspecies of the Cornflower plant, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, Centaurea gallica var. micrantha has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to reduce swelling, soothe skin irritations, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies. In herbal preparations, Centaurea gallica var. micrantha is often combined with other herbs and used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
